title: Chapter 3 - Document Structure
description: Check your understanding of the HTML document skeleton.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of <!DOCTYPE html>?

options.label: It tells the browser to use modern HTML standards
It displays a title on the page

explanations.overview: The DOCTYPE ensures the browser parses the page with the correct rules.

byOptionId.a: Correct — DOCTYPE tells the browser to use the standard HTML parsing mode.
byOptionId.b: Incorrect — DOCTYPE does not display titles.
byOptionId.c: Incorrect — CSS is linked with <link>, not DOCTYPE.
byOptionId.d: Incorrect — DOCTYPE does not define the body element.

questions.prompt: Which tag contains the visible content of the page?

explanations.overview: The <body> contains content the user sees in the browser window.

byOptionId.head: Incorrect — <head> holds metadata and resources, not the main visible content.
byOptionId.html: Incorrect — <html> is the root wrapper, not the visible content container.
byOptionId.meta: Incorrect — <meta> provides metadata such as charset or viewport info.

explanations.overview: <title> belongs in the head and appears in the browser tab.

byOptionId.head: Correct — <title> is placed in <head> and is shown in the browser tab.
byOptionId.footer: Incorrect — <footer> is part of the page body, not where <title> belongs.

questions.prompt: What is the root element of an HTML document?

explanations.overview: <html> is the outer element that wraps the whole document.

byOptionId.head: Incorrect — <head> is a child of <html>.
byOptionId.body: Incorrect — <body> is also a child of <html>, not the root.
byOptionId.doctype: Incorrect — DOCTYPE is a declaration, not the root element.
